title: Redstone Basics
aliases: what does each redstone component do
quickAnswer: Redstone starts with signal flow: dust carries power, repeaters refresh and delay it, comparators read container states, and observers detect block updates. Most common builds are combinations of those basics.
keyFacts: Signal strength usually drops along redstone dust, which is why repeaters are used to refresh lines.
Comparators are core for item filters, storage logic, and any build that reads inventory levels.
Starter circuits worth learning are a simple clock, a piston door, and an item filter because they unlock many practical builds.
commonMistakes: Copying a circuit without understanding where the signal is actually weakening or being inverted.
Using observers everywhere when a repeater or comparator would make the circuit easier to reason about.
